caption:
GoHighLevel just closed a gap most follow-up systems still have.

Here's the problem: a lead texts in, your rep jumps on the phone and answers by hand. The automated sequence has no idea a human already responded, so it keeps firing scheduled messages right on top of that conversation. The lead gets double-texted about something already handled.

As of July 8, 2026, GoHighLevel shipped a fix: a "User Replied" trigger. The instant a team member responds, the workflow can stop the sequence or skip straight to the next step. No manual tagging required.

If you run automated follow-up, ask whoever built it one question this week: does it know when your team has already answered? If the answer is no, leads are getting double-texted right now and nobody's tracking it.

Want your follow-up system checked for this exact gap?

DM "FLOW".

#LeadFollowUp #CRMAutomation #ServiceBusiness #SpeedToLead #SmallBusinessMarketing
rationale: GoHighLevel's own July 2026 changelog confirms a fix for a real, common follow-up failure, and this account hasn't covered CRM platform news before, filling the under-served News/Industry Intelligence slot with something owners can act on immediately.
